calendar.matters:
PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
Off Calendar Jul-23-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Neil Negrete, Esq., counsel for petitioner, May Wong - by telephone.

Mr. Negrete notes the primary concern of today's hearing, to wit: is there evidence to support a finding that the couple's first home was purchased with community funds or separate property funds. Mr. Negrete concedes that there is little evidence one way or the other but notes that since the couple bought the home after they were married, there is a presumption that the property was purchased with community funds. After further discussion, the Court takes the matter under submission.

Judge: Hon. Mary E. Wiss; Clerk: R. Michael Diles; Bailiff: Deputies Choi and Nuque; Reporter: Not Reported. (D306)

PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
Off Calendar Dec-09-2019 Continued To Jan-27-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: May Wong in Propria Persona.

The Court stated that a spousal property petition is kind of a short cut to probate and it is authorized, but everything has to be in order, that the petition still isn't complete and that box #7 doesn't explain why both parcels are community property, or when and how it was acquired. The Court also stated that the proposed order is entirely insufficient, that it cannot confirm her share because it is in a trust and that it can deny the petition or take it off calendar and that it thinks Ms. Wong needs to hire an Attorney. In addition, the Court stated that if Ms. Wong hires an Attorney, it can take the petition off calendar and they can start all over. Ms. Wong acknowledged that she needs to hire an Attorney, expressed concern about paying $450.00 all over again and asked for a continuance. The Court stated that it will allow one more continuance. The Petition for Spousal Property is continued to January 27, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. An amended petition is to be filed by January 17, 2020.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.

PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
Off Calendar Oct-28-2019 Continued To Dec-09-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
May Wong in Propria Persona.

Ms. Wong stated she does not understand what is needed. The Court noted that these matters can be difficult, that Ms. Wong should have an attorney, that the court cannot tell her what to do, that she needs to prove the property is community property, give notice and follow probate rules and regulations. Ms. Wong stated she talked to an attorney located in Los Angeles. The Court stated there are good local attorneys, that she can call the State Bar, and hopes she will have an attorney.

The Spousal Property Petition is continued to December 9, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Shirley Le. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.
